b2-concessive-nuance.title: "Even Though" and "Despite" (-са да, ...қарамастан)
b2-concessive-nuance.explanation: Kazakh expresses concession — "even though X, Y happens anyway" — in two common ways. Attaching the conditional -са/-се to the verb and following it with да/де ("also/even") builds "even if/though": Жаңбыр жауса да, барамын (Even if it rains, I'll go). The postposition қарамастан ("despite, regardless of") follows a nominalized clause (often with -ғанына): Суық болғанына қарамастан, шықтық (Despite it being cold, we went out).
b2-concessive-nuance.fact: These two concessive strategies aren't interchangeable in register — -са да is the natural choice in everyday speech and simple written Kazakh, while қарамастан carries a noticeably more formal, almost journalistic weight.

dialogue.en: The weather is bad, are you still going?
Even if it rains, I'll go.

b2-layered-modality.title: Layering Modal Certainty (болуы мүмкін, керек шығар)
b2-layered-modality.pattern: [clause] + болуы мүмкін (it's possible that) | [clause] + керек шығар (it probably should be)
b2-layered-modality.explanation: Advanced Kazakh often stacks two modal expressions to fine-tune certainty. Болуы мүмкін ("it may be/it's possible") attaches after a nominalized clause to hedge a claim: Ол келмеуі мүмкін (It's possible he won't come). Combining a necessity word with the particle шығар ("perhaps, it seems") softens an obligation into a guess: Ол үйде керек шығар (He's probably supposed to be at home).
b2-layered-modality.fact: Шығар is the 3rd-person future/probable form of болу ("to be"), which is why it can attach to almost any word to add a layer of "I think, but I'm not sure" — the same job as English "I guess," but grammaticalized into the modal system rather than a separate adverb.

b2-topic-focus-particles.title: Topic and Focus Particles (да/де, ғой, тек қана)
b2-topic-focus-particles.explanation: Kazakh uses a small set of particles to control which part of a sentence carries emphasis. Да/де attached to a word means "also/too," or in a negative context "even": Мен де барамын (I'm going too). Ғой can add emphatic certainty: Ол келеді ғой (He IS coming, after all). Тек қана ("only, just") placed before a word narrows focus onto exactly that element: Тек қана сен білесің (Only you know).
b2-topic-focus-particles.fact: Because these particles attach to or precede the specific word being emphasized rather than moving the whole sentence around, Kazakh can shift focus onto almost any single word just by placing a particle next to it — a flexibility a fixed-stress language like English usually achieves through vocal emphasis alone.

items.en: Even if it rains, we'll go for a walk.
Even though I was tired, I finished my work.
Even though I had little money, I traveled.
Despite it being cold, they went out.

items.kk: Қиын болса да, ол жеңіске жетті.
items.en: Even though it was difficult, he achieved victory.

items.kk: Уақыт аз болса да, біз бітірдік.
items.en: Even though there was little time, we finished.
Despite the obstacles, they achieved success.
Even though he was sick, he went to work.

items.kk: Сынға қарамастан, ол өз пікірінде қалды.
items.en: Despite the criticism, he stayed with his opinion.
It will probably be cold tomorrow.
He probably made the right decision.

items.kk: Ол тек қана жұмыс туралы ойлайды.
